NimbusVault accreditation transcript, Q4 2024. The participants are security review, release office, platform operations, and the Compose trust attestation team. The minutes include preliminary notes, superseded votes, side conversations, and final closure statements. Earlier statements were intentionally preserved because auditors asked for the full trail.

Session note 0001. Round one discussed emergency mTLS debugging for the gateway and did not approve production use. The speaker reminded the group that draft rows and mirror rows are evidence, not a final ledger. Several names recur in these notes, so the recorder warned against accepting the first matching phrase without checking the review round and closure language.

Board closure, final reading. The canonical date for expiry checks is 2024-12-20. Approved Compose security exceptions are taken from the latest review round for each exception id, not from earlier approvals. Latest statuses of withdrawn, suspended, rejected, provisional, or expired do not enter the canonical exception table. A duplicate exception for the same service and rule only survives when it has its own approved final review evidence; otherwise it remains audit context.

Board closure, mTLS and secret handling. The gateway mTLS debug exception remains approved only for nimbus-gateway in the production Compose evidence. The worker legacy secret mount was withdrawn in the last round, and the cron legacy secret candidate fails because the evidence is not read-only. The changelog signer exception is approved because the final release bundle uses the signed pipeline evidence.

Board closure, Compose refs. The release ledger uses exact docker/compose branch refs of the form refs/heads/release/major.minor. Suffix branches such as release/2.27.x and prefixed experiments such as release/security-2.29 are not canonical release branches. Changelog tags use signed Q4 releases only. Release candidates, draft changelog entries, and namespace-prefixed aliases do not become canonical tags; the signed in-toto variant normalizes to its base version when it supersedes an unsigned base tag.

Board closure, schema check. The Compose JSON schema snapshot in the evidence mirror is the remote schema check for offline replay. A candidate exception must name a service that actually appears in its Compose evidence, and the evidence must satisfy the schema shape used by the mirror before a canonical row can be written.
